prompt: a realistic microscopy image of a lab plate with bacterial colonies matching the nine shapes from the reference sketch.
prompt_refined: Generate a photorealistic microscopy image of a laboratory Petri dish. The dish should contain bacterial colonies that precisely match the nine distinct microbial shapes presented in image 1. Ensure each of the nine shapes from image 1 is represented as a separate, visible colony distributed across the plate surface.
objects: bacterial colony matching rod shape (from image 1)
bacterial colony matching bacterium with single flagellum shape (from image 1)
bacterial colony matching spherical spiky shape (from image 1, top right)
bacterial colony matching irregular amoeba-like shape (from image 1)
bacterial colony matching bacterium with multiple flagella shape (from image 1)
bacterial colony matching spiky elongated shape with internal rings (from image 1)
bacterial colony matching chain of rod shape (from image 1)
bacterial colony matching irregular spiky shape with granules (from image 1)
bacterial colony matching spherical spiky shape with internal squiggles (from image 1, middle left)
